cti_text: An adversary sent a spearphishing email with a malicious Word attachment. When opened, a VBA macro spawned powershell.exe with a base64-encoded command that downloaded a second-stage payload. Persistence was established via a Registry Run key.
should_not_match: an admin running a signed PowerShell maintenance script at logon

cti_text: The threat actor accessed LSASS memory using a renamed copy of a credential dumping tool to harvest plaintext credentials and NTLM hashes from the host.
should_match: non-standard process opening a handle to lsass.exe with read permissions
should_not_match: antivirus or EDR legitimately reading lsass for scanning

cti_text: After initial access, the actor created a scheduled task that ran a payload from the public Temp directory every hour to maintain persistence.
should_match: schtasks.exe creating a task pointing at C:\Users\Public or \Temp\
should_not_match: software installers creating legitimate update tasks in Program Files
